ACH debit of $4,872.00 posted 06/02/2026 to checking account ending 4491. Originator: PAYPALSETTLEMENT. Customer flagged unusual — no prior PayPal history on account. Three additional ACH debits from same originator in past 5 days totaling $14,200. Customer did not authorize. Recommend freeze and SAR filing.

Outbound wire of $87,500 initiated 06/01/2026 from business checking account ending 8823 to beneficiary bank in Hong Kong. Customer is a small landscaping company with no prior international wire history. Wire was initiated via online banking from an unrecognized IP. Customer unreachable by phone.

Debit card transaction of $43.17 at AMAZON.COM on 06/03/2026 flagged by customer on account ending 2201. Customer states they did not make the purchase. No other suspicious activity on account in past 90 days. Card has been blocked pending investigation.

Altered check for $12,000 presented for payment 05/30/2026 against personal checking account ending 3374. Original check was written for $120.00 to a utility company. Payee name and amount chemically altered. Check clearing returned. Customer notified.

Customer called to report suspicious activity on their account. Could not provide specifics. No transactions flagged in system review. Advised customer to monitor and call back with transaction details.

Wire transfer of $1,250,000.00 sent 05/28/2026 from commercial account ending 7701 to a law firm escrow account in New York. Customer is a real estate developer with documented history of large wire transfers. Transaction pre-authorized by two signatories per account agreement. No anomalies detected.

Five consecutive ATM withdrawals totaling $2,500 made on 06/04/2026 from savings account ending 9912 at locations across three states within a 6-hour window. Customer is known to reside in Nebraska. Withdrawals occurred in Florida, Georgia, and Tennessee. Customer did not report travel.

{"transaction_type": "other", "amount": 2500.00, "currency": "USD", "account_last4": "9912", "transaction_date": "2026-06-04", "originator": null, "fraud_indicators": ["multi-state ATM activity in short window", "geographic anomaly", "customer residence mismatch"], "customer_reported": false, "recommended_action": "contact customer immediately, consider card block", "risk_level": "high", "sar_required": false}

ACH credit of $8,300.00 received 06/01/2026 to personal checking account ending 5543. Originator listed as US TREASURY TAX REFUND. Customer has no history of refunds this size. Customer immediately initiated wire of $7,900 to an overseas account. Classic overpayment pattern suspected.

Customer reports receiving a call from someone claiming to be bank fraud department. Caller instructed customer to move $15,000 from savings account ending 6634 to a 'safe account' via wire. Customer complied on 06/03/2026 before calling bank. Wire destination was a prepaid debit account. Impersonation scam confirmed.

POS transaction of $0.99 on 06/02/2026 to account ending 3381 followed by transaction of $4,299.00 to same merchant ELECTRONICSWORLDONLINE two minutes later. Small test charge pattern typical of card testing fraud. Card not present transaction. Customer not yet contacted.

Business account ending 4402 received 14 ACH credits from 14 different originators between 05/25/2026 and 06/01/2026 totaling $43,750. Account opened 30 days ago. No business history prior to credits. Funds partially withdrawn via cash. Structuring and money mule activity suspected.

Customer disputes a recurring subscription charge of $14.99 from STREAMFLIX on 06/01/2026 to debit card on account ending 7723. Customer states they cancelled subscription in March. Merchant has continued to bill. Second dispute for same merchant in 60 days.

Counterfeit check for $22,500 deposited 05/29/2026 to business checking account ending 5510. Check drawn on a closed account at another institution. Funds partially released before counterfeit identified. Customer claims they received check as payment for equipment sold online. Classic online marketplace scam.

Wire of $3,200 sent 06/04/2026 from personal account ending 8841 to Mexico. Customer is a documented seasonal agricultural worker who regularly sends remittances to family. This is the fourth similar wire this year. No anomalies. Customer verified by phone.

Nine cash deposits totaling $18,000 made to checking account ending 2293 over 10 days between 05/22/2026 and 06/01/2026. All deposits between $1,800 and $2,100. No single deposit exceeds $2,500. Customer is a sole proprietor hair salon owner. Structuring threshold avoidance pattern identified.

Customer age 78 visited branch on 06/03/2026 requesting wire of $9,500 to an individual in Jamaica. Customer appeared confused, stated the recipient had won a prize and needed fees paid upfront. Customer could not explain relationship to recipient. Lottery scam suspected. Wire not processed pending review.

Zelle transfer of $2,800 sent from account ending 9934 on 06/02/2026 to an unregistered recipient. Customer states they were selling concert tickets on social media and buyer requested Zelle. Customer sent tickets digitally but payment never cleared — Zelle confirmation was a screenshot forgery. Social engineering scam.

Large cash withdrawal of $9,800 from account ending 5521 on 06/04/2026. Customer is 81 years old. Branch staff noted a younger individual waited in the lobby during the transaction. Customer seemed nervous and declined to explain purpose of withdrawal. Possible elder financial exploitation by caregiver or family member.

Business checking account ending 6610 received an international wire of $175,000 EUR from a shell company in Cyprus on 05/27/2026. Business is a small import/export company with average monthly revenue of $40,000 USD. No invoice or contract on file to support transaction. Funds moved to three separate accounts within 48 hours.

Debit card charge of $218.43 from UBER* EATS on 06/05/2026 disputed by customer on account ending 8802. Customer states charge is double — they were billed twice for the same order. Transaction log confirms two identical charges 3 minutes apart. Merchant error likely. Not fraud.
